TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == baduwac == 肋骨 / makudusay a tademaw sa maazih ku baduwac. siwkay nu baduwac baduwac sa u ukak nu tademaw, itiza i baluwang. midiput tu balucu' , bala', atu ilabuay nu kidu. sisiwa ku baduwac a ukak. u aadupan sa, izaw tu ku baduwac. malecad tu tademaw a kidu. tinaku a kamu sibaduwac 有肋骨 / sibaduwac ku misakabian tu dungec nu uway. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika 肋骨(拉丁語Costa,複數Costae,形容詞costalis) . In vertebrate anatomy, ribs are the long curved bones which form the rib cage. In most tetrapods, ribs surround the chest, enabling the lungs to expand and thus facilitate breathing by expanding the chest cavity. They serve to protect the lungs, heart, and other internal organs of the thorax. In some animals, especially snakes, ribs may provide support and protection for the entire body.
S.PHON: == baduwaʦ == ** / makudusaj a tadəmaw sa maaziħ ku baduwaʦ. siwkaj nu baduwaʦ baduwaʦ sa u ukak nu tadəmaw, itiza i bar~ɾuwaŋ. midiput tu bar~ɾuʦuʔ~ʡ , bar~ɾaʔ~ʡ, atu ir~ɾabuaj nu kidu. sisiwa ku baduwaʦ a ukak. u aadupan sa, izaw tu ku baduwaʦ. mar~ɾəʦad tu tadəmaw a kidu. tinaku a kamu sibaduwaʦ *** / sibaduwaʦ ku misakabian tu duŋəʦ nu uwaj. u sur~ɾit nu ħur~ɾam atu amir~ɾika **(***ʦosta,**ʦostaə,***ʦostar~ɾis) . in *ərtəbratə anatomj, ribs arə tħə r~ɾoŋ ʦur*əd bonəs wħiʦħ form tħə rib ʦagə. in most tətrapods, ribs surround tħə ʦħəst, ənabr~ɾiŋ tħə r~ɾuŋs to ə*pand and tħus faʦir~ɾitatə brəatħiŋ bj ə*pandiŋ tħə ʦħəst ʦa*itj. tħə sər*ə to protəʦt tħə r~ɾuŋs, ħəart, and otħər intərnar~ɾ organs of tħə tħora*. in somə animar~ɾs, əspəʦiar~ɾr~ɾj snakəs, ribs maj pro*idə support and protəʦtion for tħə əntirə bodj.
S.FORM: == baduwac == 肋骨 / makudusay a tademaw sa maazih ku baduwac. siwkay nu baduwac baduwac sa u ukak nu tademaw, itiza i baluwang. midiput tu balucu' , bala', atu ilabuay nu kidu. sisiwa ku baduwac a ukak. u aadupan sa, izaw tu ku baduwac. malecad tu tademaw a kidu. tinaku a kamu sibaduwac 有肋骨 / sibaduwac ku misakabian tu dungec nu uway. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika 肋骨(拉丁語Costa,複數Costae,形容詞costalis) . In vertebrate anatomy, ribs are the long curved bones which form the rib cage. In most tetrapods, ribs surround the chest, enabling the lungs to expand and thus facilitate breathing by expanding the chest cavity. They serve to protect the lungs, heart, and other internal organs of the thorax. In some animals, especially snakes, ribs may provide support and protection for the entire body.
S.PHON: == baduwaʦ == ** / makudusaj a tadəmaw sa maaziħ ku baduwaʦ. siwkaj nu baduwaʦ baduwaʦ sa u ukak nu tadəmaw, itiza i bar~ɾuwaŋ. midiput tu bar~ɾuʦuʔ~ʡ , bar~ɾaʔ~ʡ, atu ir~ɾabuaj nu kidu. sisiwa ku baduwaʦ a ukak. u aadupan sa, izaw tu ku baduwaʦ. mar~ɾəʦad tu tadəmaw a kidu. tinaku a kamu sibaduwaʦ *** / sibaduwaʦ ku misakabian tu duŋəʦ nu uwaj. u sur~ɾit nu ħur~ɾam atu amir~ɾika **(***ʦosta,**ʦostaə,***ʦostar~ɾis) . in *ərtəbratə anatomj, ribs arə tħə r~ɾoŋ ʦur*əd bonəs wħiʦħ form tħə rib ʦagə. in most tətrapods, ribs surround tħə ʦħəst, ənabr~ɾiŋ tħə r~ɾuŋs to ə*pand and tħus faʦir~ɾitatə brəatħiŋ bj ə*pandiŋ tħə ʦħəst ʦa*itj. tħə sər*ə to protəʦt tħə r~ɾuŋs, ħəart, and otħər intərnar~ɾ organs of tħə tħora*. in somə animar~ɾs, əspəʦiar~ɾr~ɾj snakəs, ribs maj pro*idə support and protəʦtion for tħə əntirə bodj.
